text:
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
The information in this document is provided as a guide only and 
is not professional advice, including legal advice. It should not be 
assumed that the guidance is comprehensive or that it provides a 
definitive answer in every case.
1
Chapter 8 - Restricted Shares
This document should be read in conjunction with sections 112, 128, 128D and 897B of 
the Taxes Consolidation Act 1997 
Document last updated November 2025
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
2
Table of Contents
8.1 Introduction ............................................................................................................3
8.2 Meaning of Restricted Shares.................................................................................4
8.3 Income Tax Charge on Acquisition of Shares..........................................................5
8.4 Abatement of Income Tax Charge ..........................................................................6
8.4.1 Shares Acquired under Revenue Approved Schemes .......................................7
8.5 Removal or Variation of Restriction........................................................................8
8.6 Disposal of Shares.................................................................................................10
8.7 Capital Gains Tax...................................................................................................11
8.8 Return of information...........................................................................................12
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
3
8.1 Introduction
Restricted shares are shares acquired by an employee or director of a company, 
including shares acquired on the exercise of a right (i.e., a share option), where a “clog” 
or restriction is placed on the disposal of those shares. Under the terms of a written 
agreement, the shares are held in a trust established by the employer for the benefit of 
the employee for a specified period of at least one year. During this period the 
employee is restricted from assigning, transferring, pledging as security for a loan, or 
selling the shares in any circumstances. 
Section 128D Taxes Consolidation Act (“TCA”) 1997 sets out the tax treatment of 
restricted shares acquired by directors and employees.   
A charge to income tax would normally arise on the difference between the market 
value of the shares at the date of acquisition and the price (if any) paid by the employee 
or director. However, to take account of the restriction placed on the employee or 
director from disposing of the shares, the amount chargeable to income tax is abated, 
by between 10% and 60%, depending on the length of the period of restriction.
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
4
8.2 Meaning of Restricted Shares
To qualify for the abatement, the shares acquired by the employee or director must:
 be shares in the company in which the employee or director is employed or in a 
company that controls (within the meaning of section 432 TCA 1997) that 
company, and
 be restricted shares.
Shares are restricted shares if all of the conditions below are satisfied. The conditionality 
applies at the time of acquisition and during the whole length of the specified period —
(a) there is a written contract or agreement in place under the terms of which there 
is a restriction on the freedom of the employee or director by whom the shares 
are held to assign, charge, transfer or otherwise dispose of the shares for a 
specified period of not less than one year,    
(b) the shares cannot be assigned, charged, transferred or otherwise disposed of 
during that specified period, except in limited circumstances.  The limited 
circumstances are:
(i) on the death of the employee or director,
(ii) where there is a change in control or a reorganisation of the share capital 
of the company in which the shares are held,
(c) the shares are held in a trust established by the employer (e.g., an employee 
benefit trust) or held under such other arrangements as the Revenue 
Commissioners may allow. The trust must be established in the State, in an EEA 
State or the United Kingdom and the trustees must be resident in the State, in an 
EEA State or in the United Kingdom.
Prior approval from Revenue is required for restricted shares to be held in any 
other arrangement that is not a trust, e.g., if they are to be held by a solicitor, or 
in a secure stockbroker account, a nominee company or any other structure.
Case specific queries regarding shares held under “other arrangements” can be 
sent to the Employee Share Scheme Section of Revenue, who can be contacted 
at shareschemesection@revenue.ie.
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
5
8.3 Income Tax Charge on Acquisition of Shares
Broadly, an employee or director may acquire shares under an employee share award 
scheme, an employee share purchase plan (ESPP), or an employee share option scheme.
An income tax charge will arise under Schedule E where the employee acquires the 
shares for less than market value.  A charge to income tax may arise in accordance with:
 Section 112 TCA 1997, in respect of shares acquired under an employee share 
award scheme, where for example, the shares are given free of charge to the 
employee or director, or under an employee share purchase plan where the 
employee or director can purchase the shares at a discount, or 
 Section 128 TCA 1997, where the employee or director acquires the shares on 
the exercise of a share option.
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
6
8.4 Abatement of Income Tax Charge
Section 128D TCA 1997 provides for an abatement of the income tax charge on the 
acquisition of the shares.  The income tax charge is to be computed by reference to the 
market value of the shares at the date of acquisition without regard to the restriction on 
disposal.
The rate of abatement then depends on the number of years for which the restriction 
on the disposal of the shares remains in place.  The Table below sets out the rates of 
abatement that apply.
Restriction Period (Years) Rate of Abatement
1 10%
2 20%
3 30%
4 40%
5 50%
More than 5 years 60%
Example 1
On 26 January 2025 an employee is awarded 1,000 ordinary shares in his employing 
company for nil consideration.  The market value of the shares at that date was €10 per 
share.   The employee is prohibited from disposing of the shares for 4 years.  All of the 
conditions outlined in Paragraph 8.2 above are met.  
Income tax charge on acquisition
Market value of shares €10,000
Amount paid by employee         Nil            
Gain to the employee €10,000
Less: Abatement (4 years restriction = 40%) (€4,000)
Amount chargeable to income tax     €6,000*
*USC and PRSI ignored for the purpose of this example.
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
7
8.4.1 Shares Acquired under Revenue Approved Schemes
The abatement does not apply to shares acquired by a director or employee under 
Revenue approved schemes (i.e., approved profit-sharing schemes (“APSS”), employee 
share ownership trust (“ESOT”) or savings-related share option (“SAYE”) schemes).
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
8
8.5 Removal or Variation of Restriction
Where the original restriction on the disposal of the shares is subsequently removed or 
varied during the specified period (for instance, reduced from 5 years to 2 years), the 
income tax charge on the acquisition of the shares must be adjusted to reflect the actual 
period the restriction was in place.
A claw back of the abatement may be necessary to collect any additional income tax 
liability which may arise as a result of the removal or variation of the restriction. Where 
the employer is responsible for operating PAYE via payroll on the initial award/share 
allotment and applies the abatement in accordance with the clog period, the employer 
will be required to account for the additional tax due on the removal or variation of 
the restriction, with effect from February 2022. See example 2 below.
Where the employer does not have an obligation to account for the tax via payroll on 
the initial share award which is subject to the clog period, the responsibility to account 
for the additional tax due following the removal or variation of the restriction will 
remain with the employee, to be accounted for via the self-assessment system. 
Example 2
On 1 March 2022, an employee is awarded 1,000 ordinary shares in his employer 
company for nil consideration.  Under the terms of the award, the shares cannot be 
disposed of for a period of 6 years.  The market value of the shares at 1 March 2022 is 
€3 per share. All of the conditions outlined in Paragraph 8.2 above are met.
On 1 March 2025, the employer removes the restriction on the disposal of the shares. 
The employer is required to account for and operate PAYE in respect of the following:   
On the 1 March 2022 share award 
Market value of shares €3,000
Amount paid by employee   Nil
Gain to the employee €3,000
Less abatement (5+ years = 60%) €1,800
Amount chargeable to income tax   €1,200*
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
9
On the removal of the restriction on 1 March 2025 - Revision of charge arising on 1 
March 2022
Market value of shares €3,000
Amount paid by employee        Nil
Gain to the employee €3,000
Abatement revised (2 years = 20%) €600
Revised amount chargeable to income tax March 2020 €2,400
Amount previously charged €1,200
Additional amount chargeable to income tax €1,200*
*USC and PRSI ignored for the purpose of this example.
In this example, the employer is obliged to account for the tax arising on the additional 
amount chargeable to tax of €1,200 - which arises due to the removal of the restrictions 
by the employer. The employer must account for the tax on this amount via the payroll. 
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
10
8.6 Disposal of Shares 
Where shares are disposed of before the end of the specified period of restriction in any 
of the limited circumstances outlined in Paragraph 8.2(b) above, that is:
- the death of the employee or director, or 
- certain change of control/reorganisation events)
then, as in the case of a removal or variation of a restriction, the income tax charge on 
the acquisition of the shares must be adjusted to take account of the actual period the 
restriction was in place. 
Any other disposal or transfer of the shares before the end of the specified period of 
restriction may result in the shares no longer being restricted shares for the purpose of 
section 128D TCA 1997.
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
11
8.7 Capital Gains Tax
An individual who acquires restricted shares is chargeable to capital gains tax (“CGT”) on 
any chargeable gain realised on the subsequent disposal of those shares. The tax is due 
on the difference between –
(a) the sale price of the shares; and
(b) the acquisition cost of the shares.
Where an amount chargeable to income tax on the acquisition of shares by a director or 
employee is to be treated as forming part of the acquisition costs of the shares for CGT 
purposes under section 552 TCA 1997, then the amount to be so treated is the abated 
amount brought into charge on the acquisition of the shares plus any additional amount 
charged as a consequence of the lifting or variation of the restrictions or in the event of 
a permitted disposal.
The acquisition cost of the shares for CGT purposes is calculated as follows:
Shares acquired on exercise of a share option
Where the shares are issued on the exercise of the share option, the cost of acquisition 
is the sum of the following:
• the cost (if any) of the option,
• the price paid for the shares on exercise of the option, and
• the amount charged to income tax on the exercise of the option taking 
account of any reduction in the amount of the charge and any additional 
amount charged on the variation or lifting of the restriction.
Where the shares are already in existence at the time of exercise of the share option the 
cost of acquisition is the market value of the shares at the time of exercise.
Shares acquired otherwise than on exercise of a share option
In the case of newly issued shares, the cost of acquisition is the sum of the following:
• the price paid for the shares, and
• the amount charged to income tax on the acquisition of the shares taking 
account of any reduction in the amount of the charge and any additional 
amount charged on the variation or lifting of the restriction.
Where the shares are already in existence, the cost of acquisition is the market value of 
the shares at the time of acquisition.
For guidance on calculating any gain on the disposal of the shares, please see How to 
calculate CGT on the Revenue website. 
Tax and Duty Manual Share Schemes Manual - Chapter 8
12
8.8 Return of information
Where restricted shares are acquired on the exercise of a share option, in accordance 
with the provisions of section 128 TCA 1997, full particulars are to be included on the 
RSS1 return on or before 31 March in the year following the year of exercise of the 
option.
In any other case, employers must report the following information on the Employer’s 
Share Awards return (Form ESA):
 Details of an award of restricted shares in the return year the award is granted.
 Details of any disposal of restricted shares before the end of the specified period 
in the return year the disposal takes place.
 Details of forfeiture of restricted shares after the end of the specified period in 
the return year the forfeiture takes place.
The Form ESA must be filed by 31 March following the relevant tax year. The return 
must be completed offline and then uploaded to Revenue Online Service (ROS). The 
return contains detailed instructions on how to complete and upload it to ROS. In 
addition, comprehensive guidance on the filing of share scheme returns can be found in 
TDM Share Scheme Reporting - Chapter 15.
